metadata.vintage_notes: Roster compiled from each lender's own website. Statewide CO coverage unless noted. Update via scripts/update-capital-partners.js (manual curation — partner programs change ~annually).

deal_types.lihtc-4pct: 4% federal LIHTC paired with tax-exempt bonds
deal_types.usda-rd: USDA Rural Development financing or preservation
deal_types.soft-debt: Soft / subordinate debt + grants (county / city funds)

partners.name: Colorado Housing and Finance Authority (CHFA)
partners.notes: Allocates 9% LIHTC + State LIHTC + PAB; first-mortgage permanent debt + tax-exempt bond financing.

partners.name: USDA Rural Development — Colorado
partners.notes: Section 515 multifamily direct + Section 521 rental assistance; key preservation source for expiring covenants.

partners.name: Colorado Department of Local Affairs (DOLA)
partners.notes: Administers Prop 123 equity grants + State Housing Fund + Housing Development Grant + workforce + Affordable Housing Development Fund (AHDF). Critical for stacking soft debt.

partners.name: Enterprise Community Partners — Mountain Plains
partners.notes: LIHTC equity syndication + bridge / acquisition loans + Section 4 grants. Active partner on most CHFA awards.

partners.name: LISC Colorado
partners.notes: Acquisition + predevelopment + bridge loans; Communities of Practice support for newer developers.

partners.name: Mercy Loan Fund (Mercy Housing)
partners.notes: Predevelopment + construction + permanent loans for affordable housing developers; particularly active on Catholic / nonprofit deals.

partners.name: Catholic Health Initiatives (CommonSpirit) — Healthy Communities Fund
partners.service_area: Communities served by CommonSpirit hospitals (CO Springs, Denver Metro, Centura system)
partners.notes: Workforce housing + community development loans; aligns to hospital workforce-housing programs.

partners.name: Cinnaire
partners.notes: LIHTC equity + tax-credit syndication; growing Colorado portfolio.

partners.name: Raymond James Affordable Housing Investments
partners.notes: Major national LIHTC equity buyer; competitive pricing.

partners.name: FHLB Topeka — Affordable Housing Program (AHP)
partners.notes: AHP grants + Community Investment Program loans. Annual competitive AHP round.

partners.name: FHLB Des Moines — Affordable Housing Program
partners.notes: AHP grants competitive round; alternative to FHLB Topeka.

partners.name: Fannie Mae Multifamily Affordable Housing
partners.notes: Permanent loans + bond execution; preferred for 4% LIHTC takeouts.

partners.name: Freddie Mac Multifamily Targeted Affordable Housing
partners.notes: TAH loans + bond execution. Direct Purchase Program is the workhorse for 4% LIHTC + preservation.

partners.name: HUD 221(d)(4) FHA Insured Multifamily
partners.notes: FHA-insured construction + 40-year permanent loan; standard for unsubsidized + LIHTC stacks.

partners.name: Pioneer Real Estate Advisors
partners.notes: Active CO co-developer + impact equity partner; resort-market focus.

partners.name: Boulder County Worthy Cause Fund
partners.notes: Voter-approved sales-tax fund for capital projects of nonprofit affordable-housing providers.

partners.name: Denver Affordable Housing Loan Fund (City)
partners.notes: Layered with Dedicated Affordable Housing Fund (DAHF); ~$25M/yr available for gap financing.
